agentSourceNotes.reason: remote-exec is an Agent profile, but its executable action space is intentionally empty here; host adapters decide which remote/system tools are exposed.
signal-analysis is a generic Agent run profile, while SignalCollector remains an Alembic daemon/product-shell service.
macOS execution depends on local native resources, TCC policy, and artifact materialization owned by the Alembic product shell.
macOS capability manifests expose host platform permissions and native screenshot behavior, not portable Agent tool contracts.

allowedCallSites.reason: Lark remains a host transport and should call the future Agent service facade instead of owning runtime internals.
HTTP route owns request/session presentation while delegating run execution to AgentService.
HTTP extraction route is an Alembic API boundary around Agent task execution.
Recipe relation discovery should later call the Agent package facade from the route boundary.
The module composes Agent runtime classes with Alembic-owned Dashboard, Skill, Mac, Terminal, Workflow, and MCP adapters.
Module scanning remains an Alembic service and should later delegate scan agent tasks to the Agent package.
SignalCollector is an Alembic daemon feature that invokes the signal-analysis Agent profile.
The V2 tool capability base currently inherits the Agent Capability type and belongs to the common tool-system migration set.
Completion workflow now consumes Agent memory from @alembic/agent/memory, but consolidation stays local until AlembicAgent publishes an explicit domain subpath.
Internal bootstrap consumers coordinate Alembic workflow state with Agent run outputs; memory types now come from @alembic/agent/memory.
Bootstrap runtime building still needs local service/runtime/prompt contracts until A6-1; context and memory are consumed from @alembic/agent/context and @alembic/agent/memory.
Bootstrap input builders assemble Alembic workflow inputs into Agent runtime inputs; MemoryCoordinator now comes from @alembic/agent/memory.
Projection code shapes AgentRunResult into Alembic workflow output.
Session execution builder uses Agent run input/result contracts at the Alembic workflow boundary.
Preparation validates host availability of AgentService and SystemRunContextFactory.
Session runner delegates dimension work to AgentService while retaining Alembic workflow orchestration.
Internal fill types expose AgentService/SystemRunContextFactory at the workflow boundary.

toolBoundaryRules.reason: Core tool contracts, routing services, decisions, envelopes, and presenters are portable Agent tool-system code.
Capability manifests and unified catalog are common Agent tool registry infrastructure.
V2 router, handlers, cache, compressor, capability catalog, and context adapter are common Agent tool runtime candidates.
Workflow registry is a generic tool-system extension point; concrete workflow handlers remain host supplied.
WorkflowAdapter is generic but must keep its handler registry injectable from the Alembic host.
TerminalAdapter is part of the common terminal abstraction but still executes against host project roots and policy.
Terminal session state is reusable common terminal execution infrastructure.
Session manager implementation is reusable terminal execution infrastructure.
Terminal executor, environment, artifact, audit, and envelope helpers are common terminal runtime pieces.
Terminal capability manifests and helpers describe portable terminal tool surfaces.
Terminal risk policy and input normalization are common tool-governance pieces.
Dashboard operation execution is an Alembic product-shell bridge, not a portable Agent tool runtime.
Dashboard operation manifests and handlers address Alembic Dashboard state and should stay host-owned.
macOS execution is host platform integration requiring native resources and local privacy boundaries.
macOS capability manifests expose host-specific platform permissions and native screenshot behavior.
Skill loading reads Alembic injectable skills and project skills from host paths.
Skill capability manifests are tied to Alembic skill packaging and host project skill files.
